title: Data Analysis & ML Pitfalls - Fill in the Blank

questions.question: Name the umbrella problem in which information from the test set influences the trained model.
questions.blank_sentence: When test-set information improperly reaches the model during training, the methodology suffers from data ___.
questions.explanation: Data leakage is when information that should be unseen at training time reaches the model, producing optimistic, untrustworthy performance estimates.

questions.question: Name the type of split that keeps all of one participant's samples on the same side of the train/test divide.
questions.blank_sentence: To prevent subject-level leakage with repeated measures, use a ___ split by participant ID.
questions.explanation: A group split assigns all records from a given subject to one side only, so the model cannot memorize a person who appears in both train and test.

questions.question: Name the split appropriate for time-series and forecasting data.
questions.blank_sentence: Forecasting data must be divided with a ___ split so the future never leaks into the past.
questions.explanation: A chronological split trains on earlier data and tests on later data, respecting temporal order; a random split lets the model interpolate future from past.

questions.question: Name the resampling technique that creates synthetic minority-class points and must be applied inside each fold.
questions.blank_sentence: Applying ___ to the whole dataset before splitting leaks synthetic points built from the test data into training.
questions.explanation: SMOTE synthesizes new minority samples from existing ones; if run before the split it builds points partly from test rows, so it must run inside each fold.

questions.question: Name the transparency violation of presenting a finding as if it had been predicted in advance.
questions.blank_sentence: Rewriting the introduction to claim an unplanned result was the original hypothesis is called ___.
acceptable_answers: Hypothesizing After the Results are Known
questions.explanation: HARKing (Hypothesizing After the Results are Known) dresses an exploratory result up as confirmatory; the fix is to label it as exploratory and note it was not pre-specified.

questions.question: Name the practice of trying many DVs, models, or metrics and reporting only the ones that reached significance.
questions.blank_sentence: Selectively reporting only the analyses that came out significant is known as ___.
questions.explanation: P-hacking inflates false positives by searching over analyses until something is significant; the fix is to report everything and pre-register confirmatory tests.

questions.question: Name the held-out partition that should be touched only once, for the final unbiased estimate.
questions.blank_sentence: Hyperparameters are tuned on validation data, but the final unbiased estimate comes from the ___ set, used only once.
questions.explanation: The test set must be locked and used a single time; repeatedly evaluating on it lets the analyst overfit the test set and learn its noise.

questions.question: Name the single statistical model that underlies t-tests, ANOVA, and regression.
questions.blank_sentence: Behavioural statistics largely rest on one model, the general ___ model, written y = X-beta + epsilon.
questions.explanation: The general linear model y = X-beta + epsilon unifies t-tests, ANOVA, and regression as the same model presented differently.

questions.question: Name the model needed for repeated-measures or nested data, which plain regression cannot handle.
questions.blank_sentence: Nested or repeated-measures data require a linear ___ model, y = X-beta + Z-u + epsilon, because plain regression assumes independence.
questions.explanation: A linear mixed model adds random effects (Z-u) for grouped data; ignoring the nesting shrinks standard errors and produces false positives.

questions.question: Name the leakage where the prediction target was computed from variables that are then kept as inputs.
questions.blank_sentence: When a depression score is the average of symptom items and those items remain as features, the study has ___ leakage.
questions.explanation: Target leakage occurs when the outcome is derived from features that stay in the input set; the model trivially reconstructs the target, so the source variables must be dropped.
